qaIssuesAddressed.description: btn-tertiary 'VIEW RESOURCE' text on resource/policy cards in dark mode becomes low-contrast (faded cream/off-white) when hovering directly on the btn-tertiary element
qaIssuesAddressed.rootCause: The dark:hover:text-off-white rule from button.css was overriding the Archive.css !important rules. The existing overrides only covered .group:hover .btn-tertiary but not standalone .btn-tertiary:hover
qaIssuesAddressed.fixApplied: Added standalone .btn-tertiary, .btn-tertiary:hover, and .btn-tertiary:focus selectors to Archive.css that force color: var(--color-charcoal) !important in the dark mode card context, regardless of group hover state

filesModified.purpose: Added standalone btn-tertiary hover overrides for dark mode resource/policy cards

instructions: Navigate to any dark mode variation (Blog Archive - Full Content, Resources Archive - Full Content, Policies Archive - Full Content)
Hover over a resource or policy card - card background should transition to accent (neon-green)
While card is in hover state, move cursor directly over the 'VIEW RESOURCE' button text
The button text should remain charcoal (dark) at all times - both during card hover and direct button hover
No faded cream/off-white text should appear at any point
Test in all three browsers (Chromium, Firefox, Webkit) at all breakpoints

technicalDetails.specificity: The fix works by adding more specific CSS selectors that match the btn-tertiary element directly within the archive dark mode card context. By including both base state (.btn-tertiary) and hover state (.btn-tertiary:hover) with !important, we ensure the charcoal color overrides any component-level dark:hover rules from button.css.
technicalDetails.selectorChain: .archive.dark .card-resource .btn-tertiary:hover (specificity: 0,4,0) beats .btn-tertiary.dark:hover (specificity from Tailwind: 0,2,0) due to higher specificity plus !important flag
technicalDetails.redundancy: Kept the existing .group:hover .btn-tertiary selectors for redundancy, though the new standalone selectors should cover all cases

notes: The fix addresses the root cause identified in DQA-001: dark:hover:text-off-white from button.css
The standalone selectors ensure btn-tertiary text stays charcoal in ALL states within dark mode cards
Light mode was already working correctly and is unaffected by this fix
No TypeScript or PHP changes needed - this is purely a CSS specificity fix
